items.title: AI Weekly: Micron boosts chip stocks, firms mull AI-related cuts
items.summary: STORY: From chip stocks getting a fresh boost... to where chip workers have become among the most desirable partners.. This is AI Weekly.:: AI WeeklyChip stocks rebounded after bullish outlooks from U.S. firms Micron Technology and Qualcomm.Micron said customers had committed to spend $22 billion on its chips.It also forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations, sending its shares 12% higher in after-hours trading.Landsberg Bennett Chief Investment Officer Michael Landsberg says the AI boom has been transformational for memory chip makers like Micron:“What’s happened in this space has been, typically this used to be more of a commodity, and so what’s happened now with AI, a lot of these chips are needed to make AI work faster and better so the demand has really been strong across the entire sector.”Donald Trump has said Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in the U.S.The move would be a major boost for the American chipmaker's efforts to turn around its business.A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker.That would boost Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC.Oracle's total workforce declined 13%, or about 21,000 employees, in its fiscal 2026.It's as the cloud computing giant continued restructuring its business... partly driven by the adoption of AI across its operations.Up to 3,000 Santander employees in Spain could also be offered early retirement.That's as the bank braces for impacts brought on by AI, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Wednesday.SK Hynix overtook Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company.Shares in what is now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker rose to bring the company's market capitalization to $1.35 trillion.  Employees at chip factories are also now part of South Korea’s A-list. Some workers at Samsung’s chip division are set for bonuses of about $416,000.Around 14 times South Korea’s average annual salary.Chip workers now rival doctors and lawyers as the nation’s most desirable partners.Lee Sung-mi from matchmaking agency Sunoo says tech staffers now rate very highly:"Scores for Samsung Electronics employees have recently risen from 83 to 87. As for clients who work at SK Hynix, their scores are expected to climb from around 80 to 83—showing just how highly favored the company has become."
items.title_kr: AI Weekly: Micron은 칩 주식을 늘리고 기업은 AI 관련 삭감을 고려합니다.

items.title: Chip stocks rebound after bullish outlooks from Micron, Qualcomm
items.summary: STORY: Chip stocks are seeing a rebound after bullish outlooks from U.S. firms Micron Technology and Qualcomm.South Korea’s tech-heavy Kospi index jumped around 5% in early trade Thursday.Chip giant SK Hynix gained around 10% by mid-morning.The rally came after days of sharp declines for global tech stocks, sparked by concerns over the sky-high valuations for AI-related shares.:: MicronThose worries eased late Wednesday when Micron said customers had committed to spend $22 billion on its chips.It also forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations, sending its shares 12% higher in after-hours trade.Landsberg Bennett Chief Investment Officer Michael Landsberg says the AI boom has been transformational for memory chip makers like Micron:“What’s happened in this space has been, typically this used to be more of a commodity, and so what’s happened now with AI, a lot of these chips are needed to make AI work faster and better so the demand has really been strong across the entire sector.”Also after the bell, Qualcomm said it expected $15 billion in sales for its data center business by 2029.That’s as it seeks to move beyond its core market of chips for smartphones and focus on AI.:: SK HynixWednesday also saw SK Hynix reveal plans for a major Wall Street stock listing that may raise close to $30 billion.That could make it the second-biggest share sale ever, coming just after the record IPO by Elon Musk’s rocket firm SpaceX.

items.title: South Korean chip shares surge after Micron flags strong AI-related demand
items.summary: South Korean semiconductor shares rallied on Thursday after U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology's quarterly results and forecast beat expectations, boosting ‌optimism over sustained demand for AI-related chips. Shares of SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics rose ‌as much as 11.6% and 6.2%, respectively, in early trade, tracking a rally in U.S. chip stocks after Micron's ​earnings and outlook reinforced confidence in the memory sector. The rally also followed SK Hynix's announcement on Wednesday of plans to raise up to 45.45 trillion won ($29.52 billion) through a secondary listing on Nasdaq, as it seeks to capitalise on strong investor appetite for AI stocks.
